title: Randia sherborniae
intro: Randia sherborniae is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Sherborn's Randia. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Randia sherborniae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Randia sherborniae is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in some traditional remedies to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
